message: Block Public Access is not configured for the account 065209282642.
status_detail: Block Public Access is not configured for the account 065209282642.

finding_info.desc: Check S3 Account Level Public Access Block.
finding_info.title: Check S3 Account Level Public Access Block.

remediation.desc: You can enable Public Access Block at the account level to prevent the exposure of your data stored in S3.

risk_details: Public access policies may be applied to sensitive data buckets.

message: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has bucket ACLs disabled.
status_detail: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has bucket ACLs disabled.

finding_info.desc: Check if S3 buckets have ACLs enabled
finding_info.title: Check if S3 buckets have ACLs enabled

remediation.desc: Ensure that S3 ACLs are disabled (BucketOwnerEnforced). Use IAM policies and bucket policies to manage access.

risk_details: S3 ACLs are a legacy access control mechanism that predates IAM. IAM and bucket policies are currently the preferred methods.

message: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has a bucket policy allowing cross account access.
status_detail: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has a bucket policy allowing cross account access.

finding_info.desc: This check verifies that S3 bucket policies are configured in a way that limits access to the intended AWS accounts only, preventing unauthorized access by external or unintended accounts.
finding_info.title: Ensure that general-purpose bucket policies restrict access to other AWS accounts.

remediation.desc: Review and update your S3 bucket policies to remove permissions that grant external AWS accounts access to critical actions and implement least privilege principles to ensure sensitive operations are restricted to trusted accounts only

risk_details: Allowing other AWS accounts to perform sensitive actions (e.g., modifying bucket policies, ACLs, or encryption settings) on your S3 buckets can lead to data exposure, unauthorized access, or misconfigurations, increasing the risk of insider threats or attacks.

message: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket does not have correct cross region replication configuration.
status_detail: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket does not have correct cross region replication configuration.

finding_info.desc: Verifying whether S3 buckets have cross-region replication enabled, ensuring data redundancy and availability across multiple AWS regions
finding_info.title: Check if S3 buckets use cross region replication.

remediation.desc: Ensure that S3 buckets have cross region replication.

risk_details: Without cross-region replication in S3 buckets, data is at risk of being lost or inaccessible if an entire region goes down, leading to potential service disruptions and data unavailability.

message: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has Server Side Encryption with AES256.
status_detail: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has Server Side Encryption with AES256.

finding_info.desc: Check if S3 buckets have default encryption (SSE) enabled or use a bucket policy to enforce it.
finding_info.title: Check if S3 buckets have default encryption (SSE) enabled or use a bucket policy to enforce it.

remediation.desc: Ensure that S3 buckets have encryption at rest enabled.

risk_details: Amazon S3 default encryption provides a way to set the default encryption behavior for an S3 bucket. This will ensure data-at-rest is encrypted.

message: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket does not have event notifications enabled.
status_detail: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket does not have event notifications enabled.

finding_info.desc: Ensure whether S3 buckets have event notifications enabled.
finding_info.title: Check if S3 buckets have event notifications enabled.
types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards/NIST 800-53 Controls

remediation.desc: Enable event notifications for all S3 general-purpose buckets to monitor important events such as object creation, deletion, tagging, and lifecycle events, ensuring visibility and quick action on relevant changes.

risk_details: Without event notifications, important actions on S3 buckets may go unnoticed, leading to missed opportunities for timely response to critical changes, such as object creation, deletion, or updates that could impact data security and availability.

message: Server Side Encryption is not configured with kms for S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket.
status_detail: Server Side Encryption is not configured with kms for S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket.

finding_info.desc: Check if S3 buckets have KMS encryption enabled.
finding_info.title: Check if S3 buckets have KMS encryption enabled.

remediation.desc: Ensure that S3 buckets have encryption at rest enabled using KMS.

risk_details: Amazon S3 KMS encryption provides a way to set the encryption behavior for an S3 bucket using a managed key. This will ensure data-at-rest is encrypted.

message: Block Public Access is not configured for the S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket.
status_detail: Block Public Access is not configured for the S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket.

finding_info.desc: Check S3 Bucket Level Public Access Block.
finding_info.title: Check S3 Bucket Level Public Access Block.

remediation.desc: You can enable Public Access Block at the bucket level to prevent the exposure of your data stored in S3.

risk_details: Public access policies may be applied to sensitive data buckets.

message: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket does not have a lifecycle configuration enabled.
status_detail: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket does not have a lifecycle configuration enabled.

finding_info.desc: Check if S3 buckets have Lifecycle configuration enabled.
finding_info.title: Check if S3 buckets have a Lifecycle configuration enabled

remediation.desc: Enable lifecycle policies on your S3 buckets to automatically manage the transition and expiration of data.

risk_details: The risks of not having lifecycle management enabled for S3 buckets include higher storage costs, unmanaged data retention, and potential non-compliance with data policies.

message: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has MFA Delete disabled.
status_detail: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has MFA Delete disabled.

finding_info.desc: Check if S3 bucket MFA Delete is not enabled.
finding_info.title: Check if S3 bucket MFA Delete is not enabled.

remediation.desc: Adding MFA delete to an S3 bucket, requires additional authentication when you change the version state of your bucket or you delete and object version adding another layer of security in the event your security credentials are compromised or unauthorized access is granted.

risk_details: Your security credentials are compromised or unauthorized access is granted.

message: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has Object Lock disabled.
status_detail: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has Object Lock disabled.

finding_info.desc: Check if S3 buckets have object lock enabled
finding_info.title: Check if S3 buckets have object lock enabled

remediation.desc: Ensure that your Amazon S3 buckets have Object Lock feature enabled in order to prevent the objects they store from being deleted.

risk_details: Store objects using a write-once-read-many (WORM) model to help you prevent objects from being deleted or overwritten for a fixed amount of time or indefinitely. That helps to prevent ransomware attacks.

message: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has versioning disabled.
status_detail: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has versioning disabled.

finding_info.desc: Check if S3 buckets have object versioning enabled
finding_info.title: Check if S3 buckets have object versioning enabled

remediation.desc: Configure versioning using the Amazon console or API for buckets with sensitive information that is changing frequently, and backup may not be enough to capture all the changes.

risk_details: With versioning, you can easily recover from both unintended user actions and application failures.

message: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket does not allow public write access in the bucket policy.
status_detail: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket does not allow public write access in the bucket policy.

finding_info.desc: Check if S3 buckets have policies which allow WRITE access.
finding_info.title: Check if S3 buckets have policies which allow WRITE access.

remediation.desc: Ensure proper bucket policy is in place with the least privilege principle applied.

risk_details: Non intended users can put objects in a given bucket.

message: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has public access due to bucket policy.
status_detail: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has public access due to bucket policy.

finding_info.desc: Ensure there are no S3 buckets open to Everyone or Any AWS user.
finding_info.title: Ensure there are no S3 buckets open to Everyone or Any AWS user.

remediation.desc: You can enable block public access settings only for access points, buckets and AWS accounts. Amazon S3 does not support block public access settings on a per-object basis. When you apply block public access settings to an account, the settings apply to all AWS Regions globally. The settings might not take effect in all Regions immediately or simultaneously, but they eventually propagate to all Regions.

risk_details: Even if you enable all possible bucket ACL options available in the Amazon S3 console the ACL alone does not allow everyone to download objects from your bucket. Depending on which option you select any user could perform some actions.

message: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket is not publicly listable.
status_detail: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket is not publicly listable.

finding_info.desc: Ensure there are no S3 buckets listable by Everyone or Any AWS customer.
finding_info.title: Ensure there are no S3 buckets listable by Everyone or Any AWS customer.

remediation.desc: You can enable block public access settings only for access points, buckets and AWS accounts. Amazon S3 does not support block public access settings on a per-object basis. When you apply block public access settings to an account, the settings apply to all AWS Regions globally. The settings might not take effect in all Regions immediately or simultaneously, but they eventually propagate to all Regions.

risk_details: Even if you enable all possible bucket ACL options available in the Amazon S3 console the ACL alone does not allow everyone to download objects from your bucket. Depending on which option you select any user could perform some actions.

message: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket is not publicly writable.
status_detail: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket is not publicly writable.

finding_info.desc: Ensure there are no S3 buckets writable by Everyone or Any AWS customer.
finding_info.title: Ensure there are no S3 buckets writable by Everyone or Any AWS customer.

remediation.desc: You can enable block public access settings only for access points, buckets and AWS accounts. Amazon S3 does not support block public access settings on a per-object basis. When you apply block public access settings to an account, the settings apply to all AWS Regions globally. The settings might not take effect in all Regions immediately or simultaneously, but they eventually propagate to all Regions.

risk_details: Even if you enable all possible bucket ACL options available in the Amazon S3 console the ACL alone does not allow everyone to download objects from your bucket. Depending on which option you select any user could perform some actions.

message: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket allows requests over insecure transport in the bucket policy.
status_detail: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket allows requests over insecure transport in the bucket policy.

finding_info.desc: Check if S3 buckets have secure transport policy.
finding_info.title: Check if S3 buckets have secure transport policy.

remediation.desc: Ensure that S3 buckets have encryption in transit enabled.

risk_details: If HTTPS is not enforced on the bucket policy, communication between clients and S3 buckets can use unencrypted HTTP. As a result, sensitive information could be transmitted in clear text over the network or internet.

message: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has server access logging disabled.
status_detail: S3 Bucket prowler-test-public-bucket has server access logging disabled.

finding_info.desc: Check if S3 buckets have server access logging enabled
finding_info.title: Check if S3 buckets have server access logging enabled

remediation.desc: Ensure that S3 buckets have Logging enabled. CloudTrail data events can be used in place of S3 bucket logging. If that is the case, this finding can be considered a false positive.

risk_details: Server access logs can assist you in security and access audits, help you learn about your customer base, and understand your Amazon S3 bill.

message: S3 bucket prowler-test-public-bucket is not a known shadow resource.
status_detail: S3 bucket prowler-test-public-bucket is not a known shadow resource.

unmapped.notes: This check is based on the 'Bucket Monopoly' vulnerability disclosed by Aqua Security.

finding_info.desc: Checks for S3 buckets with predictable names that could be hijacked by an attacker before legitimate use, leading to data leakage or other security breaches.
finding_info.title: Check for S3 buckets vulnerable to Shadow Resource Hijacking (Bucket Monopoly)

remediation.desc: Ensure that all S3 buckets associated with your AWS account are owned by your account. Be cautious of services that create buckets with predictable names. Whenever possible, pre-create these buckets in all regions to prevent hijacking.

risk_details: An attacker can pre-create S3 buckets with predictable names used by various AWS services. When a legitimate user's service attempts to use that bucket, it may inadvertently write sensitive data to the attacker-controlled bucket, leading to information disclosure, denial of service, or even remote code execution.
